native_accessibility.use_when: Visible app replay across desktop apps, browser windows, OS UI, file pickers, browser chrome, and permission prompts.

platforms.macos: Accessibility API / AX, System Events, osascript, Finder clipboard, and screencapture

terminal.use_when: Command-line workflows where shell commands and their output are the primary interface.

file_system.use_when: Direct file and folder operations that do not require interacting with a visible application.

visual_computer_use.use_when: Fallback for visible desktop interactions when native accessibility cannot reliably address the control.
visual_computer_use.avoid_when: A native accessibility or system command path can perform the same action.

guidance: Use browser DOM automation (click, type, setFiles) for page interactions. Prefer DOM file chooser injection (setFiles) when available.
Fall back to native OS file picker automation only when the browser blocks DOM file injection.
Match elements by accessible name, placeholder, label text, and role before using coordinate-based fallbacks.
Prefer platform-native accessibility controls for desktop app windows and OS UI.
On macOS use Accessibility API / AX; on Windows use UI Automation / UIA; on Linux use AT-SPI/accessibility APIs.
Replay the recorded visible app/browser directly with native desktop automation; do not switch to another app or browser unless the user approves.
Use native accessibility across browser, desktop, file picker, browser chrome, and OS-dialog steps.
On macOS, use osascript/System Events, AX inspection, Finder clipboard file paste, keyboard shortcuts, screencapture, and visual checks for browser plus OS-dialog workflows.
Use shell commands for terminal workflows and verify command output before continuing.
Use file-system operations for direct file changes.
Use native accessibility for file pickers or Finder/Explorer dialogs.
Start with structured native accessibility when available.
Fall back to visual computer-use if the surface cannot be identified.
